title: I built a small AI tool to remove “almost good” trades from my strategy
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Most of my losses came from trades that looked… fine.

Not bad setups.  
Just not clean enough.

After reviewing \~100 trades:

* early entries
* weak confirmations
* forcing breakouts

So I simplified everything:

Only trade if:

* clean breakout
* proper retest
* strong candle close

If one is missing → no trade.

Big drop in useless losses.

Problem is… hard to stay consistent manually.

So I built a small AI-based tool that scans charts and only flags these structured setups.

No more “this looks okay”.

Either it matches or it doesn’t.

If you’re building / testing similar stuff:  
[https://herculmarket.com/](https://herculmarket.com/)

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
You upload a chart (TradingView, MT4, etc.), and the tool analyzes the setup to give you a structured breakdown. Things like:

* key levels
* potential entry zones
* stop loss / take profit
* and where the setup becomes invalid

The idea isn’t to tell you what to trade, but to give you a second, more objective view of your setup. Kind of like stepping back and double-checking your reasoning before taking the trade.

comments.author: EnvironmentalBid9987
comments.body: I tried to upload a mt5 screen shot send I have reached my limit ?

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Yeah, that usually means you’ve hit the free usage limit.
There’s a cap on how many analyses you can run, especially for screenshots. You should be able to try again once it resets, or upgrade if you need more.
If it happened earlier than expected, let me know — I can take a look

replies.author: EnvironmentalBid9987
replies.body: How that was the first thing I tried to do, how can I already be at my limit I haven’t used it before

comments.author: Expensive-Badger-142
comments.body: You should make it mobile friendly, your nav is being crushed. Sounds good what real trades and how much have you made in real trading using this ?

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Yeah you’re right on the mobile part — still working on improving that, especially the nav. Appreciate you pointing it out.

For the trading side, I try to be careful with that. The tool isn’t really meant to show “how much it made”, but more to help structure decisions and avoid weak setups.
So instead of focusing on PnL, the idea is:
	•	clearer entries
	•	better invalidation
	•	more consistent execution
That’s where the real value is over time.
Curious — would mobile usability be a dealbreaker for you right now or just a nice improvement?

comments.author: Expensive-Badger-142
comments.body: Would love to test yours if you test mine ?

replies.author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
replies.body:
Yeah I’m open to that 
Always interesting to see how different tools approach the same problem.
Send it over and I’ll take a look, happy to share feedback on both sides.
